sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Avantor, Inc. (NYSE:AVTR) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Avantor, Inc. (NYSE:AVTR) reported its Q4 2025 earnings, missing EPS expectations. The company is undergoing a "Revival" program, reorganizing into two new business units: VWR distribution and services, and bioscience and medtech products, to sharpen strategic focus and improve execution. Avantor anticipates 2026 to be a year of transition and investment, with expected organic revenue growth of -2.5% to -0.5% and adjusted EPS of 77¢ to 83¢.

feed.title: Guardian Pharmacy Services, Inc. to Participate in Upcoming Raymond James Institutional Investor Conference
feed.summary: Guardian Pharmacy Services, Inc. (NYSE: GRDN) announced its participation in the upcoming Raymond James Institutional Investors Conference on Monday, March 2, at 1:40 p.m. Eastern Time. The company, a leading long-term care pharmacy services provider, will make a presentation that will be accessible via a live audio webcast and a 90-day replay. Guardian Pharmacy Services manages over 50 pharmacies nationwide, serving nearly 204,000 residents in long-term care facilities.

feed.title: Public Storage (PSA) Announces Leadership Changes and Office Relocation
feed.summary: Public Storage (PSA) has announced significant leadership changes and a relocation of its principal office from Glendale, California, to Frisco, Texas. Joseph D. Russell, Jr., President and CEO, will retire on March 31, 2026, and H. Thomas Boyle will succeed him as CEO. Additionally, Joseph D. Fisher will become President and CFO, and other key leadership promotions and board changes will take effect.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries (NYSE:MHK) Announces Earnings Results
feed.summary: Mohawk Industries (NYSE:MHK) reported mixed Q4 earnings, with adjusted EPS of $2.00 and revenue of $2.70 billion, both slightly beating consensus estimates. However, GAAP net income significantly declined to $42 million from $90 million year-over-year, highlighting a disparity between adjusted and reported earnings. The company's stock traded down following the announcement and analyst downgrades, with insiders also selling shares recently.

feed.title: Cardinal Health Board of Directors Approves Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Cardinal Health's Board of Directors has approved a quarterly dividend of $0.5107 per share. This dividend will be payable on April 15, 2026, to shareholders registered by April 1, 2026. The company is a major distributor and manufacturer of pharmaceutical, medical, and laboratory products, dedicated to improving healthcare through innovative solutions.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Group announces proposed offering of senior unsecured notes
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has announced a proposed offering of senior unsecured notes. This news follows a recent filing by Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. for a two-part senior notes offering, though the size was not disclosed. The cruise company also recently raised its quarterly dividend to $1.50 per share from $1.00.

feed.title: Keurig Dr Pepper adds two independent directors, restructures board committees
feed.summary: Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) is adding two independent directors, Amie Thuener and William Newlands, to its board and restructuring its committee organization, effective March 2. This move comes as the company prepares for the closing of its JDE Peet's acquisition and a subsequent separation into two independent companies. Thuener will join the Audit & Finance Committee, while Newlands will serve on the newly created Nominating & Governance Committee, separating the previous Remuneration & Nominating Committee into two distinct bodies.

feed.title: Expedia Group (NASDAQ:EXPE) Announces Earnings Results, Misses Estimates By $0.22 EPS
feed.summary: Expedia Group (NASDAQ:EXPE) reported quarterly earnings of $3.10 per share, falling short of analysts' consensus estimates by $0.22. Following the announcement, the stock traded down, with shares hitting $227.24 on heavy volume. Several analysts have updated their price targets and ratings on EXPE, resulting in a consensus "Hold" rating with an average price target of $292.23.

feed.title: Expedia (NASDAQ:EXPE) Beats Expectations in Strong Q4 CY2025
feed.summary: Expedia (NASDAQ:EXPE) reported better-than-expected Q4 CY2025 results, with revenue up 11.4% year-on-year to $3.55 billion and non-GAAP profit exceeding analyst estimates by 12.2%. The company also provided strong next-quarter revenue guidance, surpassing analyst expectations. Despite strong financial performance, shares traded down slightly after the report.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (ES) Eversource Energy Posts Q4 Adjusted EPS $1.12 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $1.10
feed.summary: Eversource Energy (ES) announced its Q4 adjusted EPS at $1.12 per share, surpassing FactSet's estimate of $1.10. The company also reported Q4 revenue of $3.37 billion, exceeding the FactSet estimate of $2.71 billion. This news is accompanied by recent analyst price target adjustments and dividend raises for the utility company.

feed.title: Cardinal Health to pay $0.5107 dividend per share on April 15
feed.summary: Cardinal Health (NYSE: CAH) announced its Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.5107 per share. This dividend is payable on April 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 1, 2026, and will be paid out of the company's capital surplus. This action aligns with the company's established pattern of consistent, slightly rising shareholder distributions.

feed.title: UDR expects to be a net seller in 2026
feed.summary: UDR, a REIT, anticipates being a net seller of apartments in 2026 despite expecting stronger performance. The company projects 1.5% to 2% blended lease rate growth, driven by a reduction in apartment completions and strategic lease expiration management from late 2025. While optimistic, UDR acknowledges potential challenges from muted job growth and regulatory risks, leading to a focus on selling approximately $700 million in assets.

feed.title: Keurig Dr Pepper Announces New Directors and Governance Changes
feed.summary: Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) announced the appointment of two new independent directors, Amie Thuener and William "Bill" Newlands, to its Board, effective March 2. The company is also separating its Remuneration & Nominating Committee into new Nominating & Governance and Compensation Committees to strengthen governance as it approaches the JDE Peet's acquisition closing and plans for a future separation into two independent companies. These changes aim to enhance KDP's transformation and value creation agenda, bringing diverse expertise to the Board at a pivotal time.

feed.title: Citigroup Establishes New 6.5% Series JJ Preferred Stock
feed.summary: Citigroup (C) has established a new 6.500% Fixed Rate Reset Noncumulative Preferred Stock, Series JJ, effective February 11, 2026. This move expands the bank's capital structure, utilizing diverse preferred and note securities to balance funding and capital requirements while meeting investor demand for yield. Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, rates C as "Neutral," citing strong technical momentum and positive guidance, balanced against negative operating cash flow and rising leverage.

feed.title: “Unusually low” hold rates impact Wynn Macau Ltd’s profitability in 4Q25
feed.summary: Wynn Resorts reported a 4.4% increase in 4Q25 operating revenues to US$968 million, but its Adjusted Property EBITDAR fell by 7.5% due to significantly lower casino win rates in Macau. Both Wynn Palace and Wynn Macau experienced "unusually low" hold rates, impacting profitability despite increased VIP table games turnover and mass table drop in some areas. The company's CEO, Craig Billings, highlighted continued strength in its Las Vegas operations and progress on Wynn Al Marjan Island.

feed.title: Public Storage: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Public Storage (PSA) reported strong fourth-quarter results, surpassing Wall Street expectations for both funds from operations and revenue. The real estate investment trust posted funds from operations of $4.26 per share and revenue of $1.22 billion, with analysts having projected lower figures. For the full year, the company expects funds from operations between $16.35 and $17 per share.

feed.title: Federal Realty Investment Trust: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) reported its fourth-quarter earnings, with funds from operations (FFO) of $1.84 per share, missing Wall Street estimates of $1.86 per share. Despite missing FFO expectations, the company's revenue of $336 million surpassed analysts' forecasts of $329 million. For the full year, FRT anticipates FFO to be between $7.42 and $7.52 per share.

feed.title: Arista Networks: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Arista Networks (ANET) reported strong fourth-quarter earnings, with net income of $955.8 million, or 75 cents per share, surpassing analyst expectations. Adjusted earnings were 82 cents per share. The cloud networking company also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $2.49 billion for the quarter and providing a positive outlook for the current quarter.

feed.title: Should Eastman’s 2025 Earnings Slide Test Confidence in Its Efficiency and Sustainability Strategy (EMN)?
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical Company reported a significant decline in its 2025 sales and net income, with sales dropping to US$8,752 million from US$9,382 million and net income to US$474 million from US$905 million in 2024. This performance raises questions about the effectiveness of the company's efficiency and sustainability strategies amidst market headwinds. Despite the earnings setback, Eastman increased its quarterly dividend to US$0.84 per share, signaling continued commitment to shareholders while also highlighting a tension between balance sheet resilience and potential future constraints if market pressures persist.

feed.title: Is Edison International (EIX) Using Preferred Share Redemption to Quietly Recast Its Risk Profile?
feed.summary: Edison International plans to redeem all outstanding shares of its 5.375% Fixed-Rate Reset Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series A, on March 9, 2026. This move signals a shift in the company's financing costs and balance sheet management. While this action impacts capital structure, the main investment drivers for common shareholders remain regulatory decisions and wildfire expense treatment.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty Expands Wellness Brands And Athlete Campaign As Valuation Stays Rich
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty is expanding its product offerings by introducing two new brands, SickScience and Luna Bronze, and launching the "Ulta Beauty Roster" campaign to feature professional female athletes. These initiatives aim to blend merchandise growth with a focus on wellness and community engagement. Despite these strategic moves, the company's valuation remains rich, trading approximately 35.9% above its estimated fair value.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries earnings beat by $0.02, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: Mohawk Industries (NYSE: MHK) reported strong fourth-quarter results, surpassing analyst expectations with an EPS of $2.00, $0.02 better than estimated, and revenue of $2.7 billion, exceeding the consensus of $2.68 billion. The company's stock has shown positive performance, climbing 24.79% in the last three months and 11.52% over the past year. InvestingPro rated Mohawk Industries' financial health as "good performance."

feed.title: Ingersoll: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Ingersoll Rand Inc. (IR) reported strong fourth-quarter earnings, with adjusted profit of 96 cents per share, surpassing Wall Street expectations of 91 cents. The company also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $2.09 billion for the quarter. For the full year, Ingersoll reported a profit of $581.4 million and expects future earnings between $3.45 and $3.57 per share.

feed.title: (SPSC) SPS Commerce, Inc. Expects Q1 Revenue Range $191.6M - $193.6M
feed.summary: SPS Commerce, Inc. (SPSC) anticipates its Q1 revenue to fall within the range of $191.6 million to $193.6 million. Additionally, the company projects Q1 adjusted EPS to be between $0.95 and $0.99. These financial expectations follow the announcement of Q4 revenue of $192.7 million and Q4 adjusted EPS of $1.14 per share, surpassing FactSet estimates.

feed.title: Gartner Predicts 50% Of Orgs Will Adopt Zero-Trust Data Governance by 2028
feed.summary: Gartner predicts that by 2028, 50% of organizations will adopt a zero-trust posture for data governance due to the growing volume of unverified AI-generated data. The increasing prevalence of AI output raises concerns about model collapse and intensifies regulatory demands for verifying "AI-free" data. Organizations are advised to appoint AI governance leaders, foster cross-functional collaboration, leverage existing governance policies, and adopt active metadata practices to manage these risks.

feed.title: CCORF Maintains Bank of Montreal(BMO.US) With Buy Rating, Raises Target Price to $160.66
feed.summary: CCORF analyst Matthew Lee has reaffirmed a "buy" rating for Bank of Montreal (BMO.US) and increased the target price from $148.13 to $160.66. According to TipRanks, Matthew Lee has a 70.9% success rate and a 21.8% average return over the past year. This report highlights an analyst's updated recommendation and target price for the financial institution.

feed.title: Wynn Resorts Logs Mixed Fourth Quarter on Macau Strength, Las Vegas Challenges
feed.summary: Wynn Resorts reported a mixed fourth quarter, showing improvement in its Macau operations but facing ongoing struggles in its Las Vegas resorts. The gaming operator posted a profit of $100.0 million, or 82 cents per share, which is a decrease compared to the $277.0 million, or $2.29 per share, reported in the same period last year. Sales increased in Macau but declined in Las Vegas and Boston.

feed.title: Vertex: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals Inc. reported fourth-quarter net income of $1.19 billion, or $4.65 per share, with adjusted earnings of $5.03 per share. This fell short of Wall Street's expectation of $5.07 per share. Despite missing profit estimates, the company's revenue of $3.19 billion surpassed forecasts.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (IR) Ingersoll Rand Inc. Posts Q4 Adjusted EPS $0.96 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $0.90
feed.summary: Ingersoll Rand Inc. (IR) reported a Q4 adjusted EPS of $0.96 per share, exceeding the FactSet estimate of $0.90. The company also announced Q4 revenue of $2.09 billion, surpassing the FactSet estimate of $2.04 billion. Additionally, Ingersoll Rand expects its 2026 adjusted EPS to range from $3.45 to $3.57, compared to FactSet's estimated $3.54.

feed.title: Dexcom beats Street in Q4 on 13% sales growth
feed.summary: Dexcom reported strong fourth-quarter results, surpassing analyst expectations with profits of $267.3 million and sales of $1.259 billion, marking a 13% increase. Key highlights include the launch of the Dexcom G7 15 Day CGM and FDA clearance for its smart CGM-integrated basal dosing optimizer. Following the announcement, Dexcom's shares saw a 1% rise in post-market trading.

feed.title: Jefferies Adjusts Paycom Software (PAYC) Price Target to $130
feed.summary: Jefferies has maintained a "Hold" rating on Paycom Software (PAYC) but significantly lowered its price target to $130 from $190, a 31.58% reduction. This adjustment follows similar price target reductions by other analysts, reflecting a cautious outlook for the company, which provides human capital management software-as-a-service primarily to midsize U.S. businesses. Despite these adjustments, the average target price from 17 analysts suggests a 41.42% upside from the current price, with GuruFocus estimating a 129.79% upside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: JP Morgan Lowers Price Target for Global Payments (GPN) to $90
feed.summary: JP Morgan has maintained a Neutral rating for Global Payments (GPN) but lowered its price target from $95 to $90, reflecting a 5.26% decrease. This adjustment comes amidst various recent analyst actions, including new initiations, upgrades, and other price target modifications from firms like Cantor Fitzgerald, Seaport Global, and Truist Securities. Despite the lowered target, the average analyst price target for GPN is $102.42, implying a significant upside from its current price.

feed.title: FIS: JP Morgan Lowers Price Target, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: JP Morgan has reiterated its "Overweight" rating for Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) but reduced its price target from $80.00 to $65.00, an 18.75% decrease. This adjustment, made by analyst Tien-Tsin Huang, reflects a cautious but generally optimistic sentiment among analysts regarding FIS's market conditions. Despite the lowered price target, the average analyst target price of $78.70 suggests a significant upside of 67.39% from its current price, with a consensus "Outperform" recommendation from brokerage firms.

feed.title: AMETEK, Inc. (AME) Announces 10% Increase in Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: AMETEK, Inc. (AME) has announced that its Board of Directors approved a 10% increase in its quarterly cash dividend, raising it from $0.31 to $0.34 per share. This decision signals the company's strong financial health and confidence in future growth, aiming to enhance shareholder returns and attract value investors seeking reliable income streams.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (FRT) Federal Realty Investment Trust Reports Q4 Revenue $336.0M
feed.summary: Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) reported its Q4 revenue at $336.0 million. This financial update follows an earlier announcement that the company posted Q4 Core FFO of $1.84, aligning with FactSet estimates. The report also highlights recent capital recycling activities including property sales and acquisitions, alongside analyst rating adjustments.

feed.title: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. to Participate in Upcoming Investor Conference
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. announced its participation in the BofA 2026 Global Agriculture and Materials Conference on Wednesday, February 25, 2026, at 9:45 am ET. Investors can access a live webcast and replay on the company's investor relations website. The company aims to provide clean energy by decarbonizing its ammonia production network for low-carbon hydrogen and nitrogen products.

feed.title: Travere Therapeutics to Report Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: Travere Therapeutics, Inc. announced it will report its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results on Thursday, February 19, 2026, after the U.S. financial markets close. The company will host a conference call and webcast at 4:30 p.m. ET to discuss these results and provide a business update. This event will be accessible via their investor page, with an archived version available for 30 days.

feed.title: Travere Therapeutics to Report Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: Travere Therapeutics, Inc. announced it will report its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results on Thursday, February 19, 2026, after market close. The company will also host a conference call and webcast to discuss these results and provide a business update. Information for accessing the webcast and call will be available on the Investor page of Travere's website.

feed.title: Zebra Technologies Corp. Cl A stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
feed.summary: Shares of Zebra Technologies Corp. Cl A (ZBRA) rose 8.58% to $274.15 on Thursday, outperforming the broader market which saw the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average decline. This gain broke a two-day losing streak for the stock.

feed.title: Jefferies Financial Group Lowers Paycom Software (NYSE:PAYC) Price Target to $130.00
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has re-evaluated Paycom Software (NYSE:PAYC), lowering its price target to $130 from $190 while maintaining a "hold" rating, citing cautious sentiment despite a slight EPS beat in Q4. This adjustment comes amidst a recent revenue miss and a weak FY-2026 revenue outlook that has led to several analyst downgrades and a decline in share value. The stock is currently trading around $119.46, with other firms also adjusting their targets, reflecting increased near-term volatility.

feed.title: SciSparc Sets Up $10 Million Convertible Note Facility With Warrants
feed.summary: SciSparc Ltd. (SPRC) has established a $10 million convertible note facility with an institutional investor, including an initial $2.5 million note and attached three-year warrants. The funds are designated for working capital and general corporate purposes, with the option for additional tranches every three months. These notes bear 4% annual interest and are convertible into ordinary shares, which could lead to future equity dilution.

feed.title: Albemarle Earnings: Shares Sell Off Despite Strong Profit Growth Outlook Above Consensus
feed.summary: Albemarle's shares fell 7% despite fourth-quarter earnings showing improved sequential lithium prices and a management outlook above consensus estimates. Morningstar maintains its $200 fair value estimate for Albemarle, viewing it as slightly undervalued with a Very High Uncertainty Rating. The company anticipates substantially improved profits and free cash flow in 2026 due to higher lithium prices, even with reduced medium-term volume growth assumptions.

feed.title: Public Storage earnings beat by $0.10, revenue was in line with estimates
feed.summary: Public Storage (NYSE: PSA) reported fourth quarter EPS of $2.60, exceeding analyst estimates by $0.10, with revenue of $1.22 billion matching consensus. The company's stock has increased by 6.60% in the past three months, though it is down 2.94% over the last year. InvestingPro rated Public Storage's financial health as "good performance."

feed.title: Cango Secures $75.5 Million in Equity Deals to Fund AI and Bitcoin Infrastructure Expansion
feed.summary: Cango Inc., a Bitcoin miner, has secured $75.5 million in equity deals to expand its AI and computing infrastructure. This includes a $10.5 million Class B equity investment from Enduring Wealth Capital Limited, increasing their stake and voting power, and $65 million in Class A investments from its chairman and director, pending approvals. These financings will bolster Cango's balance sheet for its digital infrastructure strategy, despite mixed financial performance and technical challenges noted by Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst.

feed.title: Applied Materials shares pop on upbeat guidance, strong fiscal first quarter report
feed.summary: Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) reported robust fiscal first-quarter results, surpassing Wall Street expectations and issuing strong guidance for Q2. The company, a semiconductor equipment maker, saw its shares rise more than 9% in after-hours trading. Key highlights include record DRAM revenue in its Semiconductor Systems segment and record services revenue from Applied Global Services, driven by accelerated investments in AI computing.

feed.title: IBM 2026 Hiring Plan Triples Entry-Level Jobs, Adapts Roles for AI - News and Statistics
feed.summary: IBM plans to triple its entry-level hiring in the United States in 2026, adapting job descriptions for an AI-centric future. The company's chief human resources officer, Nickle LaMoreaux, emphasizes that these new roles will focus on tasks that complement AI capabilities, such as customer interaction for software developers and refining HR chatbot responses. This strategy aims to prevent a future shortage of mid-level managers and ensures new hires bring distinct value in an evolving job market despite concerns about AI's impact on entry-level opportunities.

feed.title: DexCom: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: DexCom Inc. reported strong fourth-quarter earnings and revenue, surpassing Wall Street expectations. The medical device company posted earnings of 68 cents per share on revenue of $1.26 billion for the quarter. DexCom also provided a full-year revenue forecast ranging from $5.16 billion to $5.25 billion.

feed.title: Arista Networks: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Arista Networks reported strong fourth-quarter results, with net income of $955.8 million, or 75 cents per share, surpassing analyst expectations. Adjusted earnings were 82 cents per share, and revenue reached $2.49 billion, also exceeding forecasts. For the full year, the company posted a profit of $3.51 billion on $9.01 billion in revenue, and anticipates current quarter revenue around $2.6 billion.

feed.title: Eversource Energy earnings missed, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: Eversource Energy (NYSE: ES) reported its Q4 earnings, with an EPS of $1.12, missing analyst estimates of $1.13. However, the company's revenue for the quarter reached $3.37 billion, surpassing the consensus estimate of $2.94 billion. Despite the earnings miss, Eversource Energy provided a positive outlook, forecasting FY 2026 EPS to be between $4.80 and $4.95.

feed.title: Baxter International: Debt, Margin Pressure, Dreadful FY26 Guidance (Downgrade) (NYSE:BAX)
feed.summary: Baxter International recently posted weak Q4 results and a disappointing FY 2026 guide, leading to a downgrade to a hold rating. Despite a cheap valuation, the company faces ongoing margin compression, significant debt risks, and repeated earnings misses, making a near-term recovery unlikely. While some segments like Advanced Surgery show growth, overall operational efficiency and profitability are severely challenged, and technical indicators suggest a prolonged downtrend for the stock.
